headline: Eli Lilly (LLY) Valuation Check As Orforglipron Launch And GLP‑1 Expansion Drive Growth Story
summary: Eli Lilly (LLY) is back in focus after a cluster of obesity and diabetes updates, from the planned global launch of oral weight loss pill orforglipron to expanded GLP-1 access through its new Employer Connect platform. See our latest analysis for Eli Lilly. The share price has eased recently, with a 30 day share price return of a 4.9% decline and a 90 day share price return of a 6.2% decline, even as high profile launches, trial readouts and obesity ecosystem moves keep attention on Eli...

headline: HSBC downgrades Eli Lilly, says shares are ‘priced to perfection’
summary: Investing.com -- HSBC has downgraded Eli Lilly to Reduce, arguing in a note on Tuesday that expectations for the fast-growing obesity drug market have become overly optimistic and that the company’s shares now look “priced to perfection.”

content.title: HSBC downgrades Eli Lilly, says shares are ‘priced to perfection’

headline: Eli Lilly Falls 5% — FDA Oral Obesity Pill Decision and Novo Nordisk Rivalry Weigh on Shares
summary: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) stock is down 5% in Tuesday afternoon trading, with shares falling to $937 from a prior close of $989.12. The move adds to a rough stretch for the stock, which is now down 13% year to date from $1,072.90 at the start of 2026. The selloff reflects a convergence of pressures: mounting ... headline: Why One Analyst Thinks LLY Is Worth $850 While the Rest of Wall Street Targets $1,216
summary: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) shares have pulled back sharply, falling 9% over the past month, and 13% year-to-date. Most Wall Street analysts remain constructive, with a consensus price target of $1,216.93 and 24 buy-equivalent ratings versus just 1 strong sell. Now, HSBC analyst Rajesh Kumar is stepping against that tide, downgrading LLY to Reduce with a ... Why One Analyst Thinks LLY Is Worth $850 While the Rest of Wall Street Targets $1,216

headline: Neurocrine Biosciences Appoints Andrew Ratz, Ph.D., as Chief Technical Operations Officer
summary: Neurocrine Biosciences, Inc. (Nasdaq: NBIX) today announced the promotion of Andrew Ratz, Ph.D., to the executive management team as the Chief Technical Operations Officer. In his new role, Dr. Ratz will lead the company's global technical development, manufacturing, and supply chain functions, supporting Neurocrine's expansion beyond small molecules into biologics and device-based therapies.

content.title: Neurocrine Biosciences Appoints Andrew Ratz, Ph.D., as Chief Technical Operations Officer
content.summary: Neurocrine Biosciences, Inc. (Nasdaq: NBIX) today announced the promotion of Andrew Ratz, Ph.D., to the executive management team as the Chief Technical Operations Officer. In his new role, Dr. Ratz will lead the company's global technical development, manufacturing, and supply chain functions, supporting Neurocrine's expansion beyond small molecules into biologics and device-based therapies.

headline: Stocks to Watch Today: Uber, Hyundai, Lululemon
summary: ↗️ Uber Technology (UBER): The ride-hailing company’s stock jumped 5% after Uber and Nvidia said Monday they were expanding their self-driving-vehicle partnership. ↗️ Hyundai Motor (KR:005380), Kia (KR:000270), Nissan Motor (JP:7201), Isuzu Motors (JP: 7202), Geely Automotive (HK:175): Shares of the Asian auto makers all moved higher after Nvidia said Monday it was expanding its autonomous-driving partnership with them.

headline: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
summary: 1515 ET – For obesity-drug makers, success will be defined by price rather than product differentiation, HSBC analysts say.  Pricing competition is set to heat up between Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk, the analysts say.  Lilly’s current guidance — which is more optimistic than Novo Nordisk’s — implies it will get a surge in GLP-1 demand when it cuts prices.

content.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
content.summary: 1515 ET – For obesity-drug makers, success will be defined by price rather than product differentiation, HSBC analysts say.  Pricing competition is set to heat up between Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk, the analysts say.  Lilly’s current guidance — which is more optimistic than Novo Nordisk’s — implies it will get a surge in GLP-1 demand when it cuts prices.
